name: Newton's Cradle
description: Demonstrates conservation of momentum and energy using a classic pendulum-ball setup.
subject: Physics
educational_goal: Show how motion transfers through the balls in a Newton's Cradle.

scene.scene: A physics lab table with a Newton's Cradle centered in frame

prompt_bundle.image_prompt: Static setup of a Newton's Cradle before motion begins.
prompt_bundle.motion_prompt: Pull the leftmost ball back and release it smoothly.
prompt_bundle.constraints: Keep the motion realistic and physically aligned
prompt_bundle.stop_condition: When the leftmost ball reaches the center and the collision begins
prompt_bundle.negative_prompt: No extra objects, no camera shake

scene.scene: The same Newton's Cradle after the first collision, with energy transferring through the balls
scene.camera: Same angle as previous clip for continuity

prompt_bundle.image_prompt: Continue the Newton's Cradle sequence from the prior frame.
prompt_bundle.motion_prompt: Show the opposite ball swinging outward after the collision.
prompt_bundle.constraints: Preserve alignment and keep the frame stable
prompt_bundle.stop_condition: When the rightmost ball reaches its peak arc
prompt_bundle.negative_prompt: No camera movement, no scene changes
